analysis.title: Detailed Analysis
analysis.summaryBuy: Most factors support buying (score {{score}}). Consider an entry with the risk management from the Trade Setup panel.
analysis.summarySell: Most factors are pressuring the price (score {{score}}). Avoid new long entries — consider taking profit or waiting for stabilization.
analysis.summaryHold: Bullish and bearish factors are still balanced (score {{score}}). Wait for stronger confirmation before entering.

factors.insufficientData: Not enough candle data ({{candles}} points) for a full analysis.
factors.rsiOversold: RSI {{rsi}} is in the oversold zone (<30) — selling pressure is overdone, bounce potential.
factors.rsiOverbought: RSI {{rsi}} is in the overbought zone (>70) — the rally is overheated, correction risk.
factors.rsiRecovering: RSI {{rsi}} is turning up from the lower area — selling pressure is easing.
factors.rsiWeakening: RSI {{rsi}} is turning down from the upper area — buying pressure is fading.
factors.rsiNeutral: RSI {{rsi}} is in the neutral zone — no extreme pressure either way.
factors.maGoldenCross: MA7 just crossed above MA25 (golden cross) — start of upward momentum.
factors.maDeathCross: MA7 just crossed below MA25 (death cross) — start of downward momentum.
factors.maBullish: MA7 is {{gap}}% above MA25 — short-term trend is up.
factors.maBearish: MA7 is {{gap}}% below MA25 — short-term trend is down.
factors.maFlat: MA7 and MA25 are overlapping — short-term trend is flat.
factors.priceAboveMa25: Price is {{gap}}% above MA25 — medium-term bias is positive.
factors.priceBelowMa25: Price is {{gap}}% below MA25 — medium-term bias is negative.
factors.priceAtMa25: Price is hugging MA25 — the market is undecided.
factors.macdBullishRising: MACD histogram is positive and expanding — buying momentum is building.
factors.macdBullishFading: MACD histogram is positive but shrinking — buying momentum is running out of steam.
factors.macdBearishFalling: MACD histogram is negative and expanding — selling momentum is building.
factors.macdBearishFading: MACD histogram is negative but narrowing — selling pressure is easing.
factors.momentumUp: Price rose {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — short-term momentum is positive.
factors.momentumDown: Price moved {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — short-term momentum is negative.
factors.momentumFlat: Price only moved {{roc}}% over the last 7 candles — momentum is flat.
factors.nearSupport: Price is only {{dist}}% above the 20-candle low — near support, limited downside room.
factors.nearResistance: Price is only {{dist}}% below the 20-candle high — near resistance, limited upside room.
factors.midRange: Price is mid-range over 20 candles ({{low}}% from support, {{high}}% to resistance) — far from both bounds.

setup.holdDesc: Composite score {{score}} — bullish and bearish factors are still balanced (needs ±30 for a signal). Per-factor detail is in the Detailed Analysis panel.
setup.leverageUnclear: Signal isn't clear yet — avoid leverage
setup.leverageModerate: Score {{score}}, {{aligned}} aligned factors — moderate signal, keep position size in check
setup.avoidTouching: Avoid letting price touch this level
setup.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Execute on your own exchange. SL is based on the last 20-candle swing — adjust to your own risk appetite.
setup.positionSizingDesc: Risks {{riskPct}}% of your portfolio ({{amount}}) if the stop-loss is hit.
setup.useThisSize: Use This Size in Paper Trade

backtest.title: Signal Backtest
backtest.subtitle: Simulates BUY/SELL signal performance against daily historical price data — see whether this strategy is actually profitable before trusting it.
backtest.noTrades: Not enough BUY/SELL signals in this historical data period yet.
backtest.disclaimer: Backtested over {{days}} days of daily historical price data, signals computed without look-ahead bias (using only data up to that day). Past performance does not guarantee future results.

tooltip.unrealizedGain: Unrealized profit or loss — the difference between current market value and total capital invested.
tooltip.cagr: Compound Annual Growth Rate — your portfolio's annualized growth rate based on historical data.
tooltip.rebalancingScore: A 0–100 score showing how well your asset allocation matches your risk profile target. Score ≥85 = optimal.
tooltip.avgCost: Average price per unit paid, accounting for all purchases and top-ups.
tooltip.capital: Total capital invested (buy price × units), before fees or taxes.
tooltip.realizedGain: Profit already realized from selling assets — locked in and will not change.
tooltip.staleAsset: This asset's price hasn't been updated this month. Update the price for accurate calculations.

projections.title: CAGR Simulator
projections.subtitle: Wealth accumulation projection based on portfolio data and monthly contributions.
projections.chartSubtitle: Based on current portfolio value + {{monthly}}/month contribution
projections.disclaimer: CAGR is an estimate — not a guarantee. Optimistic ({{optimistic}}%) and pessimistic ({{pessimistic}}%) scenarios show the range of possibilities based on historical asset class volatility.
projections.historicalBlendDesc: Rate is calculated from a blend of your historical portfolio performance over {{months}} months and the expected return model per asset class.
projections.allocationBasedDesc: Rate is calculated from the weighted average of expected return per asset class based on your current portfolio allocation.
projections.riskProfileBasedDesc: Not enough portfolio data to calculate CAGR from history or composition. Rate is taken from the {{profile}} risk profile benchmark.

goals.title: Financial Goals
goals.subtitle: Set target amounts and track their progress against your portfolio value.
goals.basis: Progress is measured against your current total portfolio value ({{value}}) assuming {{rate}}% CAGR per year.
goals.emptyDesc: Create your first goal — retirement fund, house down payment, or emergency fund — and see whether your portfolio is on track to reach it.
goals.deleteDesc: Delete “{{name}}”? This action cannot be undone.

card.calc1: Your portfolio is currently {{current}}, projected to grow {{rate}}%/year over {{months}} months → becomes ≈ {{result}}.
card.calc2: Plus a recurring {{monthly}}/month contribution over {{months}} months (compounding from the day it's deposited) → adds ≈ {{result}}.
card.calcNoContribution: You haven't set a monthly contribution (Rp 0) — this projection is purely from the growth of your existing investment, with no added contribution.
card.calc3: Total projected portfolio value at that month = {{portfolioFv}} + {{contributionFv}} = {{total}}.
card.calc4: Since {{earlier}} is already "earmarked" for earlier-priority goals, what's left for this goal = {{total}} − {{earlier}} = {{result}} — that's the "Projected at deadline" figure.
card.calc5: For the total portfolio to exactly reach the cumulative target of {{cumulative}} by {{date}}, you need a TOTAL contribution (combined across all goals) of ≈ {{required}}/month starting now.

form.name: Goal Name
form.namePlaceholder: e.g. Retirement Fund, House Down Payment
form.targetDateHint: Used to project whether you are on track.
form.monthlyContributionMoved: Monthly contribution is now a single global setting (not per goal) — set it at the top of the Financial Goals page, shared across all goals.

goals.basisMulti: Goals are ordered by nearest deadline — your current portfolio value ({{value}}) is allocated sequentially (waterfall) assuming {{rate}}% CAGR per year, so no rupiah is counted twice.

roadmap.title: Achievement Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Order of goal completion by deadline priority. Goal #1 is funded first; further contributions and growth flow to the next goal.

advice.increaseTotal: Raise your total monthly contribution to {{amount}} (currently {{current}}) to hit every deadline. The most demanding goal: {{name}}.
advice.allOnTrack: With your current contributions and growth, every goal with a deadline is on track. Keep the rhythm going.
advice.focusNext: Earlier goals are already covered by your portfolio value — your contributions are now effectively working toward “{{name}}”.
advice.addContribution: Without monthly contributions, some goals will stay out of reach from portfolio growth alone. Set a planned monthly contribution on each goal.
advice.deadlinePassed: The deadline for “{{name}}” has passed without being reached — update its date or adjust the target amount.

methodology.step1: 1) All goals are ordered by nearest deadline (a waterfall model). The same portfolio value is used sequentially: goal #1 gets "filled" first, the remainder is then counted toward goal #2, and so on — so a single rupiah is never counted twice for two different goals.
methodology.step2: 2) For each goal, the system projects the portfolio value forward using monthly compound interest: current value × (1 + monthly growth rate) raised to the number of months, plus the monthly contribution which also compounds. The growth rate currently used: {{rate}}%/year (from your portfolio's historical CAGR, same as the CAGR Simulator page).
methodology.step3: 3) That projection is compared against the cumulative target (this goal plus every earlier-priority goal) to determine the On Track / Falling Behind status.
methodology.step4: 4) "Required per month" is worked out backward from the same formula: the TOTAL monthly contribution (combined across all goals) that makes the projection land exactly on the cumulative target by the deadline.

help.title: Feature Guide
help.subtitle: Interactive simulations of every Trajectory feature — click a feature below to see how it works.

demos.title: Add Asset
steps: List of active assets — tap New Position to record a new asset
Fill in the name, purchase price per unit, lot size, and transaction date
The asset appears in the list instantly, value & gain computed automatically

demos.title: Dashboard
steps: Summary of total value, unrealized gain, CAGR, and rebalancing score
Growth chart of value vs. capital across all historical data
Rebalancing audit: actual vs. target allocation with adjustment suggestions

demos.title: Price Sync
steps: Assets marked ⚠ Stale mean their price hasn't been updated this month
The Market tab shows real-time IDX stock prices from Yahoo Finance
Click Sync — a Price Update entry is created automatically, the stale mark disappears

demos.title: Robo Advisor
steps: The AI has real-time access to all of your portfolio data
Ask anything: analysis, recommendations, or scenario simulations
The AI's response includes specific analysis based on your actual portfolio

demos.title: CAGR Simulator
steps: Enter your portfolio value, target CAGR, and investment horizon
Three scenarios are projected: optimistic, base case, and pessimistic
See estimated value 5, 10, 20, up to 30 years ahead

Trailing 12-month yield, consistency, and payout history appear automatically
Add it to your watchlist to track several issuers at once

steps: Real-time crypto watchlist from CoinGecko — price, volume, market cap
A multi-indicator scanner (RSI, MA, MACD, support/resistance) scores buy/sell signals with a per-factor reason

quickStart.title: Log your first asset
quickStart.desc: Open Portfolio → click "New Position". Fill in the asset name, category, purchase price, unit count, and date. Every asset class is supported: stocks, mutual funds, bonds, gold, crypto, even cash.

quickStart.title: Update prices monthly
quickStart.desc: For IDX stocks: open the asset detail → Market tab → click Sync. For other assets: add a "Price Update" entry. This keeps your portfolio value accurate and clears the stale ⚠ mark.

quickStart.title: Check your Dashboard
quickStart.desc: The Dashboard summarizes total value, unrealized gain/loss, estimated CAGR, rebalancing score, and a growth chart. Once a month after updating prices is enough.

quickStart.title: Ask the Robo Advisor
quickStart.desc: The AI has full access to your portfolio data. Ask for recommendations, allocation analysis, or scenario simulations any time — no need to copy-paste data manually.

entryTypes.desc: Purchase of an asset not yet in your portfolio
Additional purchase of an asset you already hold
Sale of part of an asset's units
Sale of all units, closing the position
Record the current market price (required monthly for non-cash assets)
Platform fees, taxes, or other charges
Void a previous incorrect entry without deleting it

proTips: Update prices at the start of the month so the portfolio growth chart and rebalancing score stay accurate.
Export a CSV from the Portfolio page at year-end for tax reporting.
Made a mistake? Use Edit Log (the pencil icon on an asset's detail page) — the old entry is corrected automatically and the audit trail stays intact.
For IDX stocks, the Sync button on the Market tab creates a Price Update entry with the current price instantly — no manual input needed.
Cash assets (savings, deposits) don't need monthly price updates — the system never marks them stale.
Turn on "Portfolio Context for AI" in Settings so the Robo Advisor can analyze your actual data.
Add at least 2 stocks to your Dividends & Coupons watchlist to see the Dividend Rotation Roadmap — a suggested rotation order based on historical dividend and price patterns.

Set your Total Monthly Contribution once on the Financial Goals page (not per goal) — the system automatically calculates the on-track status for all your goals and can explain the math step by step via the "See how this is calculated" button.
Check the Signal Backtest tab on a coin's detail page before trusting a BUY/SELL signal — see its historical win rate & avg return first, don't enter blind.
Create a price or RSI alert on a coin's detail page (bell icon) so you don't have to keep watching the app — notifications fire automatically while the tab is open.
glossary: Compound Annual Growth Rate — the portfolio's annualized compounded growth rate.
Paper profit on an asset that hasn't been sold yet.
Average purchase price per unit, adjusted every time you top up.
Total capital invested in an asset (units × avg cost × exchange rate).
Adjusting asset proportions back to the target allocation.
Relative Strength Index — a 0–100 momentum indicator; <30 oversold, >70 overbought.
Moving Average Convergence Divergence — the difference between EMA12 and EMA26; a strengthening positive histogram signals rising buy momentum.
A probabilistic simulation using thousands of random scenarios to estimate price.
Historical price levels where price has often reversed direction.
Dividend return: total dividends over 12 months ÷ share price × 100%.
An asset whose price hasn't been updated this calendar month — needs a Price Update or Sync.
An immutable transaction record — entries are never deleted, only corrected.
Crypto trading simulation with a virtual balance, no real money involved.
A strategy of holding a stock only around its dividend payout window to capture yield, then rotating into another stock.

items.q: How do I add a new asset?
items.a: Click "New Position" on the Portfolio page. Choose the asset category, fill in the name (and ticker for IDX stocks, e.g. BBCA), platform, purchase price per unit, unit count, currency, and date. The system automatically computes cost basis and average purchase price.

items.q: What's the difference between Top Up and New Position?
items.a: "New Position" is used when the asset isn't in your portfolio yet. "Top Up" is used for an additional purchase of an asset you already hold — the system automatically updates the weighted average purchase price.

items.q: What does a stale asset mean and how do I fix it?
items.a: An asset is marked stale (⚠ icon) if its price hasn't been updated this calendar month. For IDX stocks: open the asset detail → Market tab → click Sync. For other assets: add a "Price Update" entry with the current price. Cash assets are never marked stale.

items.q: How do I edit an entry that's already recorded?
items.a: Open the asset detail → click the pencil icon (✏) next to the entry you want to change. Fill in the fields that need changing, then save. The system marks the old entry as "corrected" and creates a new one — the audit trail stays intact.

items.q: How do I delete an asset?
items.a: Open the asset detail, scroll down, and click "Delete Asset". All related entries will also be deleted. If the asset has already been fully sold, it's better to use a "Full Sell" entry instead so the history is preserved.

items.q: Where does the Est. CAGR figure on the Dashboard come from?
items.a: CAGR is calculated from your own portfolio's historical data: (current value / first value)^(1/years) - 1. If your portfolio history is less than 1 year, the estimate uses an annualized growth rate instead.

items.q: Why does the portfolio growth chart look flat?
items.a: The chart uses historical data backfilled from your transaction entries. For IDX stocks with a ticker, historical prices are pulled from Yahoo Finance automatically. If there's no ticker or market data is unavailable, the chart falls back to the last purchase price.

items.q: What is the Rebalancing Score?
items.a: A 0–100 score showing how closely your actual asset allocation matches the target allocation for your risk profile. A score of 100 means the allocation is exactly on target. Any significant deviation from target reduces the score. The target allocation can be changed via Settings → Risk Profile.

items.q: How do I see real-time IDX stock prices?
items.a: Open the detail page of an IDX stock asset (make sure the ticker is filled in, e.g. BBCA), then click the Market tab. The price panel shows the latest price from Yahoo Finance. Click "Sync" to automatically create a Price Update entry.

items.q: What is the Next Close Estimate?
items.a: The system runs 2,000 Monte Carlo simulations using a Geometric Brownian Motion model based on historical volatility and returns. The result is a probabilistic range: a low estimate (bear, P10), a middle estimate (base, median), and a high estimate (bull, P90).

items.q: What do RSI, Bollinger Bands, and MA mean in technical analysis?
items.a: RSI (Relative Strength Index): 0–100 momentum, <30 oversold, >70 overbought. Bollinger Bands: a volatility channel above/below the MA — price outside the band suggests a potential reversal. Moving Average 20 & 50: short- and medium-term trend.

items.q: What can the Robo Advisor do?
items.a: The Robo Advisor is an AI with real-time access to your portfolio data: all assets, values, allocation, risk profile, unrealized gain/loss, and more. You can ask for analysis, rebalancing recommendations, asset suggestions, projections, or general financial questions.

items.q: Can the AI change my portfolio data?
items.a: Not directly. The AI can only recommend a change to your risk profile or target allocation — if you approve it, the Robo Advisor updates your settings. The AI cannot create or delete transaction entries.

items.q: How do I enable portfolio context for the AI?
items.a: Go to Settings → turn on "Include portfolio data for AI". When enabled, every conversation includes a snapshot of your asset data as context. When disabled, the AI only answers general questions without personal data.

items.q: What is the Dividend Rotation Roadmap?
items.a: A feature on the Dividends & Coupons page that lays out an estimated "move from stock A to stock B" order across the year, based on the historical pattern of dividend payout months and price movement around those periods. It appears automatically once you have at least 2 stocks in your watchlist.

items.q: Are the dates in the Dividend Rotation Roadmap guaranteed?
items.a: No. Yahoo Finance (our data source) only provides historical payment dates, not official cum-date/ex-date figures. So the month ranges in the roadmap are estimates based on prior years' patterns — always check the issuer's official schedule (IDX/shareholder meeting) before trading.

items.q: How is the confidence score calculated?
items.a: Based on how consistently the stock has paid dividends in the same month over the last 5 years. High = a very consistent pattern; Low = an unstable pattern, so the estimated date and return are less reliable.

items.q: How is financial goal progress calculated?
items.a: Goals are ordered by nearest deadline (a waterfall model). Portfolio value is allocated sequentially: goal #1 is funded first, the remainder flows to goal #2, and so on. Each goal's progress = the funds allocated to it ÷ the goal amount. That way, a single rupiah is never counted toward two goals at once.

items.q: What do the On Track / Falling Behind statuses mean?
items.a: If a goal has a deadline, the system projects the portfolio's value on that date using a CAGR assumption (the same one used on the CAGR Simulator page) plus the TOTAL monthly contribution from all goals. The projection is compared against the cumulative target (that goal plus every earlier-priority goal). Projection ≥ cumulative target = On Track; below it = Falling Behind.

items.q: What is the Achievement Roadmap, and why does my second goal show 0%?
items.a: The Roadmap appears once you have more than one goal: it shows the order of achievement, the estimated month each goal is reached, and suggestions (e.g. the total contribution needed). A lower-priority goal can show 0% because your portfolio value is still fully allocated to the goal with the nearer deadline — once that goal is covered, allocation automatically flows to the next one.

items.q: What's the difference between "Required per month" and the monthly contribution I entered?
items.a: Monthly contribution is now a SINGLE global figure you set once on the Financial Goals page (no longer per goal) — representing a recurring deposit into one shared portfolio. "Required per month" on each goal card is the system's calculation: the TOTAL monthly contribution (combined, not per goal) needed for that goal's cumulative target to be reached exactly by its deadline. If the required figure is well above what you've set, you'll need to contribute more, push back the deadline, or lower the target amount. Click "See how this is calculated" on any goal card for a step-by-step breakdown using your actual numbers.

items.q: Why does the goal I set during onboarding show up here?
items.a: The savings goal you entered during onboarding is saved as your first financial goal. You can change its name, amount, deadline, and contribution any time from the Financial Goals page.

items.q: How does Risk Profile affect the app?
items.a: Risk Profile determines the ideal target asset allocation used for the Rebalancing Score and Dashboard suggestions. Conservative = more bonds/gold; Aggressive = more stocks/crypto. It can be changed any time in Settings.

items.q: How do I change the app language?
items.a: Go to Settings → Account section → click the ID / EN button to switch between Bahasa Indonesia and English.

items.q: Is my data safe?
items.a: Data is stored in Firebase Firestore with security rules ensuring only you can read and write your own data (verified via Firebase Auth). All communication is encrypted over HTTPS with security headers enabled.

items.q: What is Signal Backtest and why does it matter?
items.a: Signal Backtest simulates the RSI+MA+MACD signal's performance against 200 days of daily historical price data — computing win rate, average return, and max drawdown from past BUY/SELL signals. Signals are computed without look-ahead bias (using only data up to that day). It helps you judge whether this strategy actually has an edge before trusting it for real entries.

items.q: How do Price & RSI Alerts work?
items.a: Click the bell icon on a coin's detail page, choose a metric (Price or RSI), a condition (above/below), and a threshold value. The system checks this condition every 60 seconds (price) or 5 minutes (RSI) while the app tab is open, then sends a browser notification + in-app toast once the condition is met. The alert automatically deactivates after firing once.

items.q: Do alerts still work if the app is closed?
items.a: Not yet. Alerts currently only work while the app tab is open in your browser (foreground). Push notifications while the app is closed require additional infrastructure that hasn't been implemented yet.

items.q: How is risk-based Position Sizing calculated?
items.a: On the Trade Setup panel, pick a risk percentage (1-3% of your total portfolio). The system computes: the rupiah amount you're willing to lose = risk% × portfolio value, then position size = that amount ÷ stop-loss distance (%). If leverage is used, the margin needed = position size ÷ leverage. The result can be used directly in the Paper Trading panel via the "Use This Size" button.

items.q: What is Paper Trading and how do I use it?
items.a: Paper Trading simulates buying/selling crypto with a virtual balance — practice your strategy without real money at risk. Enter an amount (or use the Position Sizing suggestion), pick a leverage, then execute. Trade history is saved per coin so you can evaluate your strategy's performance over time.

chat.title: Robo Advisor
chat.subtitle: AI powered by {{model}} · real-time
chat.noApiKeyDesc: Add VITE_AI_API_KEY, VITE_AI_API_URL, and VITE_AI_MODEL to .env then restart the dev server.
chat.pageSubtitle: Discuss your portfolio with an AI that has real-time access to your data.
chat.confirmNote: Profile & allocation changes only apply after you confirm the AI's suggestion.

advisory.title: Rebalancing Audit
advisory.subtitle: Analysis of how well your portfolio allocation matches your {{profile}} risk profile.
advisory.increaseDesc: Your {{category}} position is currently {{actual}}%, below the ideal target ({{target}}%). Recommendation: add around {{amount}}.
advisory.decreaseDesc: {{category}} is overweight at {{actual}}% (target {{target}}%). Consider taking profit of around {{amount}} to keep your risk profile in check.
advisory.balancedDesc: No rebalancing recommendations at this time.

tour.welcomeDesc: This quick tour will guide you through all the main features of the platform. It takes about 2 minutes.
tour.dashboardStatsDesc: Here you can see your total portfolio value, unrealized gain/loss, and estimated CAGR at a glance.
tour.rebalancingScoreDesc: This score shows how balanced your portfolio is compared to your ideal allocation target. Click to see detailed recommendations.
tour.portfolioDesc: The Portfolio page displays all your assets. Add new positions, view details, and manage each asset easily.
tour.advisoryDesc: The Rebalancing page analyzes your portfolio composition vs target allocation and provides specific action recommendations.
tour.projectionsDesc: Project your portfolio's future growth based on historical CAGR and your monthly contribution targets.
tour.chatDesc: Ask the AI about your portfolio status, request rebalancing recommendations, or change your risk profile directly via chat.
tour.journalDesc: All transaction history is stored here — purchases, price updates, dividends, fees, and corrections.
tour.settingsDesc: Change your name, risk profile, allocation targets, language, and theme from the Settings page.
tour.helpDesc: Find complete documentation on every feature, including this tour, on the Guide page.
tour.finishDesc: You now know all of Trajectory's features. Start adding your first position, or replay this tour anytime from the Guide page.
tour.tradingDesc: Monitor real-time crypto prices, view RSI & Moving Average signals, and run paper trades — zero risk simulation.
tour.incomeDesc: Track IDX stock dividend schedules, add tickers to your watchlist, and see historical yield data directly from Yahoo Finance.
tour.stockLiveDesc: For IDX stocks, open the Market tab in asset detail to see real-time prices and intraday charts. Use the Sync button to write the market price to your ledger.

flow.title: Investor Flow
flow.subtitle: Technical estimate · not official IDX foreign/domestic data
flow.error: Data not available for this stock.
flow.disclaimer: Data is calculated from daily price & volume (Yahoo Finance). CMF, MFI, and OBV are technical proxies for institutional money flow — not official IDX foreign/domestic segment data. Real-time IDX data requires a paid service.
flow.tip_cmf:
Chaikin Money Flow (CMF)

Measures whether volume is higher when price rises (buying) or falls (selling) over 20 days.

> +15 = strong accumulation
−15 to +15 = neutral
< −15 = strong distribution
flow.tip_mfi:
Money Flow Index (MFI)

A volume-weighted RSI. Measures the speed and strength of money flowing in or out.

> 70 = overbought
< 30 = oversold
30–70 = normal
flow.tip_obv:
On-Balance Volume (OBV)

Adds volume on up days and subtracts on down days. A rising OBV trend means buying pressure is dominating cumulatively.
flow.tip_buypct:
Buy Pressure (%)

How close the closing price is to the day's high.

100% = closed at high (all buyers)
0% = closed at low (all sellers)
>55% = buy pressure dominant
flow.tip_netflow:
Net Money Flow

Estimated net buy/sell pressure per session, calculated from close position in day's range × volume × price.

Green bar = net buy
Red bar = net sell
Blue line = 10-session cumulative
flow.tip_signal:
Composite Signal

Calculated from 4 factors: CMF, MFI, OBV trend, and the ratio of accumulation vs distribution sessions in the last 10 days.

Signal strength is shown by the boxes next to the badge.
flow.tip_scorecard:
Daily Scorecard

Buy% = close position within day's range
CMF = CMF value for that day
▲ = accumulation session
▼ = distribution session
– = neutral
